skill.name: ai-video-generation
skill.description: Generate AI videos on RunComfy via the `runcomfy` CLI — a smart router across the full video-model catalog: HappyHorse 1.0 (Arena #1, native in-pass audio), Wan-AI Wan 2-7 (open weights, audio-driven lip-sync), ByteDance Seedance v2 / 1-5 / 1-0 (multi-modal cinematic), Kling 3.0 / 2-6, Google Veo 3-1, MiniMax Hailuo 2-3, ByteDance Dreamina 3-0. Covers text-to-video (t2v), image-to-video (i2v), and Veo's video-extend endpoint. The skill picks the right model for the user's intent (Arena-#1 quality, multi-shot character identity, in-pass audio, cinematic motion, fastest path, sub-15s clip, longest duration) and ships each model's documented prompting patterns plus the minimal `runcomfy run` invoke. Triggers on "generate video", "make a video", "text to video", "t2v", "image to video", "i2v", "animate", "AI video", "make X move", "video from prompt", "video from image", or any explicit ask to produce a video clip from prompt or still.
skill.summary: Smart router across RunComfy's full video-model catalog — picks the right model for t2v, i2v, or video-extend and applies documented prompting patterns via the `runcomfy` CLI.
skill.author: doany-ai

security_audit.summary: This is a documentation/routing skill for the RunComfy CLI video model catalog. All 117 'external_commands' static findings are legitimate `runcomfy run` invocations in code examples, scoped by `allowed-tools: Bash(runcomfy *)` which restricts execution to only the runcomfy CLI. All 61 'network' findings are documentation URLs pointing to runcomfy.com, docs.runcomfy.com, and skills.sh — no data exfiltration patterns. The 14 'weak cryptographic algorithm' and 16 'system reconnaissance' findings are false positives matching patterns in markdown documentation text (e.g., the word 'seed' in schema tables, model names), not actual cryptographic code or system commands. The skill explicitly documents its security model: token stored at ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json with mode 0600, allowlisted outbound endpoints (model-api.runcomfy.net), 2 GiB download cap, and no telemetry. No prompt injection, no credential harvesting, no unauthorized network access detected.

low_findings.title: Bash execution scoped to single CLI
low_findings.description: The skill declares `allowed-tools: Bash(runcomfy *)` which restricts agent execution to the runcomfy CLI. This is good security practice — it prevents the skill from being used as a vehicle for arbitrary command execution. Verified across all code blocks: every invocation is `runcomfy run <model_id>` or install-time `npm i -g @runcomfy/cli`.

low_findings.title: Outbound network restricted to RunComfy allowlist
low_findings.description: The skill documents an explicit allowlist of outbound endpoints: `model-api.runcomfy.net` and `*.runcomfy.net` / `*.runcomfy.com`. No telemetry or callback URLs detected. User-provided reference URLs (image_url, audio_url) are documented as untrusted with indirect prompt injection warnings.

low_findings.title: Token storage follows standard practice
low_findings.description: API token is stored at `~/.config/runcomfy/token.json` with file mode 0600 (owner read/write only). Environment variable override (`RUNCOMFY_TOKEN`) supported for CI. No token echo, logging, or commit practices documented.

content.user_title: Generate AI Videos from Text or Images
content.value_statement: Choosing the right video model from dozens of RunComfy endpoints is hard. This skill routes your request to the best model for your intent and applies documented prompting patterns for quality results.
actual_capabilities: Route text-to-video requests to the best RunComfy model based on intent (quality, speed, audio, cinematic style)
Route image-to-video (i2v) requests across HappyHorse, Veo 3-1, Kling 3.0, Hailuo, and Dreamina models
Extend existing video clips using Veo 3-1's video-extend endpoint
Apply documented prompting patterns for each model (motion language, camera direction, audio description)
Invoke `runcomfy run` with correct JSON input schema for each model endpoint
Handle model selection logic across 15+ video model endpoints in the RunComfy catalog
limitations: Requires a RunComfy account and API token (sign in via `runcomfy login`)
Generation cost varies by model — premium models (Kling 3.0 4K, Seedance v2 Pro) are more expensive per clip
Single file download cap of 2 GiB enforced by the CLI
Reference image/audio URLs from third parties are untrusted and may carry indirect prompt injection

use_cases.title: Social media content creation
use_cases.description: Create vertical (9:16) video clips for TikTok, Reels, and YouTube Shorts from a text prompt or still image with in-pass audio.
use_cases.target_user: Social media managers and content creators

use_cases.title: Product showcase videos
use_cases.description: Animate product photos with physics-accurate 180-degree rotations using Veo 3-1 for e-commerce listings and brand presentations.

use_cases.title: Cinematic ad production
use_cases.description: Generate anamorphic 21:9 cinematic frames with multi-modal conditioning (up to 9 reference images, 3 video, 3 audio) using Seedance v2 Pro.
use_cases.target_user: Ad agencies and film production teams

prompt_templates.title: Simple text-to-video (beginner)
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a video: 'A golden retriever runs through a sunlit meadow, daisies bouncing, gentle breeze. Audio: birdsong, soft rustle.' Use HappyHorse 1.0 t2v, 8 seconds, 16:9, 1080p.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick social media clip from a single sentence

prompt_templates.title: Image-to-video with motion direction
prompt_templates.prompt: Animate this image: [URL]. The subject slowly turns to face the camera and smiles. Camera stays still. Audio: gentle ambient. Use HappyHorse 1.0 i2v, 6 seconds, 9:16.
prompt_templates.scenario: Animate a portrait or product still with explicit camera direction

prompt_templates.title: Multi-modal cinematic (advanced)
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a cinematic 21:9 ad frame using Seedance v2 Pro. Subject from ref image 1, mood from ref video 2, score from ref audio 1. 'Anamorphic 35mm shot, vintage car on coastal road at dusk, lens flares, Kodak 5219 grade.' 10 seconds.
prompt_templates.scenario: Ad agency production with multiple reference assets

prompt_templates.title: Audio-driven lip-sync
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a talking-head clip using Wan 2-7. 'Studio portrait of a professional speaker, soft window light.' Audio from: [voiceover.mp3 URL]. 6 seconds. The audio drives the mouth — do not transcribe dialog into the prompt.

output_examples.input: Request: 'Make a 6-second vertical video of a cat stretching in sunlight with birdsong audio'
output_examples.output: Routes to HappyHorse 1.0 i2v (default for i2v with in-pass audio). Generates 9:16, 1080p, 6-second clip with described audio synthesized in-pass. Output saved to ./out.

output_examples.input: Request: 'Animate my product photo with a 180-degree rotation, keep it stable'
output_examples.output: Routes to Veo 3-1 i2v. Prompt: 'The product slowly rotates 180 degrees on a white surface, soft daylight, no other motion.' Physics-accurate rotation, object permanence preserved.

output_examples.input: Request: 'Continue my existing Veo clip for another 8 seconds with the same lighting'
output_examples.output: Routes to Veo 3-1 extend-video. Uses the existing clip as the anchor and generates a continuation matching motion, lighting, and identity.

best_practices: Always describe motion language (verbs) before visual adjectives — 'tumbles', 'drifts', 'pushes in' are more effective than 'looks beautiful'
For i2v, describe what changes in the scene — the image is your scene, the prompt is your direction
Anchor the camera explicitly ('Camera stays still', 'slow push in') to prevent unwanted drift
anti_patterns: Do not transcribe dialog into the prompt when using audio-driven lip-sync — it fights the audio track
Do not use HappyHorse 1.0 for physics-accurate object motion — use Veo 3-1 instead
Do not use Veo for audio-driven dialog — use Wan 2-7 or HappyHorse instead

faq.question: Which model should I use by default?
faq.answer: Use HappyHorse 1.0 for both t2v and i2v. It is currently number 1 on the Artificial Analysis Video Arena, supports in-pass audio, and handles most use cases well.

faq.question: How do I generate a video from an image?
faq.answer: Use any i2v route (HappyHorse, Veo, Kling, Hailuo, or Dreamina). Pass the image URL via the `image_url` field in the `--input` JSON and describe the motion you want.

faq.question: What is the difference between t2v and i2v?
faq.answer: Text-to-video (t2v) generates a clip from a text prompt only. Image-to-video (i2v) animates a still image you provide, using your prompt to describe the motion direction.

faq.question: How do I make a video with audio?
faq.answer: Use HappyHorse 1.0 for in-pass audio generation — describe the audio in your prompt (e.g., 'Audio: wind, gulls, distant laughter'). For lip-sync to a specific voiceover file, use Wan 2-7 with the `audio_url` field.

faq.question: Can I extend an existing video?
faq.answer: Yes, use the Veo 3-1 extend-video endpoint. It continues an existing Veo clip with consistent motion, lighting, and identity. See the `video-extend` skill for chained extensions.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill only contacts `model-api.runcomfy.net` and related RunComfy domains. Your API token is stored locally with mode 0600. There is no telemetry. Reference image URLs from third parties are treated as untrusted.
